## Project context recap

- **Priorities, strict order:** correctness ŌåÆ performance ŌåÆ SRP.
- **No backward compatibility requirements.** Breaking changes acceptable.
- **Bootstrap caveat.** Execution sessions use `Edit` + `Bash: dotnet build` + `compile_check`,
  NOT `*_apply` self-application. See `self-edit-bootstrap-mode-mcp-development`.

## v2 scope discipline applied

- **Rule 1 (default 1 row = 1 initiative):** Of ~14 candidate bundles examined from v1,
  only **4 survive** strict 4-condition verification. All other rows are 1-row initiatives.
- **Rule 3 (Ōēż4 production files):** All initiatives respect this; most touch 1-2 files.
- **Rule 4 (Ōēż3 test files):** All initiatives Ōēż1 test file.
- **Rule 5 (Ōēż80K context tokens):** Range 25KŌĆō80K; only the heroic P2 sits at 80K.

# P3-correctness initiatives (sorted by cost ASC)

## I-01 ┬Ę `format-range-preview-empty-diff-bundle`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Status** | `merged (PR #165, 2026-04-16)` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `format-range-preview-empty-diff-compile-check-filter-false-clean` (P3, Defect A only ŌĆö Defect B closed v1.18.2 by `compile-check-zero-projects-claimed-success-after-reload`); `dr-9-12-flag-format-range-empty-returns-empty-diff-on-d` (P4) |
| **Diagnosis** | **Bundle Rule 1 verification:** Both rows describe the same defect: `format_range_preview` returns `unifiedDiff` with header but no `@@` hunks while apply still mutates the file. Same code path: `RefactoringService.PreviewFormatRangeAsync`. Same fix: format the whole document and splice the requested line range from the formatter's output back into the original text. Per Rule 1 c2: ONE change to ONE function resolves both. Per c3: regression tests (dirty fixture, empty preview, then apply, assert diff matches) cover both. |
| **Approach** | Replaced the range-restricted `Formatter.FormatAsync(document, [span], ŌĆ”)` with whole-document format + new `SpliceFormattedRange` helper that swaps formatter output for lines inside `[startLine, endLine]` and keeps original bytes outside. Apply now matches preview's `unifiedDiff`. Implementation in `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/RefactoringService.cs`. |
| **Scope** | 1 production file, 1 test file (extended) |
| **Estimated context cost** | 35000 |
| **Risks** | Defect A may incidentally have been fixed by v1.18.2 compile_check work ŌĆö re-verified on execute (still reproducible). |
| **Validation** | Regression tests added: `FormatRangePreview_DirtyRange_EmitsNonEmptyDiff_ApplyMatches`, `FormatRangePreview_DirtyOutsideRange_LeavesUnrelatedRegionAlone`. `dotnet build -p:TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`. `verify-release.ps1`. |
| **Performance review** | N/A ŌĆö correctness fix. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`format_range_preview` no longer returns empty diff for dirty input.** See CHANGELOG.md `[Unreleased]` Fixed section. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 2 rows. |

## I-02 ┬Ę `change-signature-preview-brace-concat-on-add-op`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Status** | `merged (PR #166, 2026-04-16)` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `change-signature-preview-brace-concat-on-add-op` (P3) |
| **Diagnosis** | `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ChangeSignatureService.cs:75-105` ŌĆö `PreviewAddParameterAsync` builds the new parameter via `SyntaxFactory.Parameter(...).WithType(SyntaxFactory.ParseTypeName(request.ParameterType + " "))`. Trailing-space hack tries to force a space between type and identifier; doesn't address the broader issue that raw factory builds nodes without proper trivia. Result on Jellyfin `MusicManager.GetInstantMixFromSong` (audit 2026-04-16 ┬¦9.1): signature line concatenates with body's `{` AND original `{` line is removed; param-separator spacing stripped. Also: `paramText` local at line 75 is computed but never read (dead local). |
| **Approach** | In `PreviewAddParameterAsync`: switch to `SyntaxFactory.ParseParameter(paramText)` (parser-built, not raw factory) ŌĆö produces properly-tokenized parameter. Use the existing `paramText` local. Drop trailing-space hack on `ParseTypeName`. In `ApplyAddRemoveAsync` line 215: preserve close-paren trailing trivia when replacing ParameterList (`newParamList.WithTriviaFrom(mds.ParameterList)`). |
| **Scope** | 1 production file (ChangeSignatureService.cs), 1 test file added |
| **Estimated context cost** | 40000 |
| **Risks** | Trivia preservation must handle both same-line and multi-line method declarations. |
| **Validation** | Regression test: `change_signature_preview op=add` with `defaultValue=default`; assert preview diff has signature on its own line, `{` on the next line, parameter has proper spaces. |
| **Performance review** | N/A ŌĆö correctness fix. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`change_signature_preview op=add` no longer concatenates the method body brace onto the signature line (`change-signature-preview-brace-concat-on-add-op`).** Switched to `SyntaxFactory.ParseParameter` for proper tokenization; preserved close-paren trivia via `WithTriviaFrom` on the new ParameterList. Dead local at `ChangeSignatureService.cs:75` removed. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. |

| **Scope** | 2 production files, 1 test file added |
| **Estimated context cost** | 40000 |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Changed ŌĆö BREAKING` |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 3 rows. |

# Heroic-last initiative

## I-44 ┬Ę `csproj-reserialization-msbuildworkspace` `schedule: heroic-last`

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| **Status** | `pending` |
| **Backlog rows closed** | `create-file-apply-csproj-side-effect-all-projects` (P2) |
| **Diagnosis** | `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/RefactoringService.cs:691-840` ŌĆö `PersistDocumentSetChangesAsync` calls `_workspace.TryApplyChanges(workspaceId, modifiedSolution)` at line 793. v1.18.2 Item #5 fix snapshots only **SDK-style csprojs with ADDED documents** (line 716-737) and restores them at line 801-824. The P2 reports a different scope: ALL 40 csprojs get BOM, blank-line stripping, CRLFŌåÆLF conversion ŌĆö even csprojs with NO document changes. Root cause: MSBuildWorkspace's internal save logic runs on every project on `TryApplyChanges`. |
| **Approach** | Extend Item #5's snapshot pattern to **every csproj in the workspace** before `TryApplyChanges`. After the call, restore csprojs whose disk content differs ONLY in normalization (BOM, line-endings, blank-line collapsing). New `Helpers/CsprojSemanticEquality.cs` parses XML on both sides; if only-trivia diff: restore. If semantic diff: keep new bytes (legitimate change). Alternative: investigate `MSBuildWorkspace.SkipUnchangedFiles` or equivalent setting; if exists, opt in. |
| **Scope** | 2 production files (RefactoringService.cs + new CsprojSemanticEquality.cs helper), 1 test file added |
| **Estimated context cost** | 80000 |
| **Risks** | Snapshotting all csprojs adds I/O; semantic XML helper must be conservative; some legitimate apply paths DO modify all csprojs (e.g., `migrate_package_preview` apply across CPM); if Roslyn-internal setting exists fix is small, else substantial. |
| **Validation** | Regression: `create_file_apply` for ONE file in ONE project of a 40-project solution; assert `git diff --stat` shows only the new file. Re-run Jellyfin ┬¦9.3 reproduction. |
| **Performance review** | **Hot path ŌĆö measure.** Snapshot/compare adds <500ms on 40-project Jellyfin expected. |
| **CHANGELOG category** | `Fixed` |
| **CHANGELOG entry (draft)** | **`*_apply` no longer reserializes unrelated csprojs (`create-file-apply-csproj-side-effect-all-projects`).** Snapshot pattern extended to every csproj; csprojs whose XML differs only in trivia restored from pre-apply bytes via new `CsprojSemanticEquality` helper. |
| **Backlog sync** | Close 1 row. P2 cleared. |
